title: Clientele & Testimonials
subtitle: Organisations we've helped across process excellence, fundraise, and government liaison.

testimonials.quote: We were running operations on WhatsApp and memory. TwelfthKey helped us document workflows, set weekly review rhythm, and define who owns what. Within weeks, daily follow-ups reduced and delivery became predictable.
testimonials.author: Founder, Augrev, Hospitality Consultancy and a Tech Provider

testimonials.quote: Our biggest issue wasn't effort—it was rework. We fixed handoffs, approvals, and reporting so decisions weren't stuck. The team finally had one version of truth and fewer last-minute escalations.
testimonials.author: Founder, Universal Education, Study Abroad Business

testimonials.quote: Government liaison work can get stuck because nobody knows what's pending, with whom, and what the next step is. TwelfthKey brought a clear tracker and a follow-up cadence, so the project moved forward without daily firefighting.
testimonials.author: Founder, CAV Projects, EPC / Infrastructure Projects

successStories.headline: From firefighting to weekly control
successStories.problem: Delivery commitments slipped, ad-hoc follow-ups were high, and the founder became the bottleneck for decisions.
successStories.whatWeDid: Mapped the end-to-end workflow, defined clear ownership, set a weekly governance rhythm, and introduced simple KPI reporting.
successStories.result: Predictable execution, fewer escalations, and clearer accountability across teams.

successStories.headline: Reduced rework by fixing handoffs
successStories.problem: Work kept looping back due to unclear inputs, ownership, and approvals between teams.
successStories.whatWeDid: Standardised inputs/outputs per step, set approval checkpoints, and implemented a single tracker for handoffs and status.
successStories.result: Less rework, faster closures, and fewer last-minute escalations.

successStories.headline: Reduced founder dependency with clear ownership
successStories.problem: Execution relied heavily on the founder; priorities changed mid-week and decisions waited for follow-ups.
successStories.whatWeDid: Defined role-wise ownership, introduced weekly planning + review, and created a simple priority board to keep the team aligned.
successStories.result: Faster decision-making, smoother execution, and reduced day-to-day dependency on the founder.

successStories.headline: Runway clarity and a fundraise-ready narrative
successStories.problem: Unclear fundraise timing and amount, and investor conversations were starting without a tight story and numbers.
successStories.whatWeDid: Clarified runway assumptions, structured the raise thesis (why now, why this amount), and tightened the narrative and financials for investor readiness.
successStories.result: Clearer decision-making on timing, better-prepared investor conversations, and a more confident fundraise plan.
